Case title: Maternal-health interview coding with generative AI
Short description: Use semistructured maternal-health interviews to test what is gained and lost when GenAI performs inductive coding and theme extraction.
Candidate roles tested: Construct boundary setter; situated interlocutor; context witness
Key typology question: Do human roles narrow differently in applied thematic analysis than in contested political-text coding?
Observable artifacts / traces: Structured prompts, generated themes, human–AI code comparisons, coding memos
Key outcomes observed: Theme overlap, coding efficiency, missing contextual nuance, interpretive adequacy
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: LLM coding captures nuance and downstream interpretive usefulness as well as human coding
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Applied health interviews may not generalize to more theory-laden or politically contested domains
Why this case has high leverage: Useful counterpoint to political-text cases because the topic is applied, interview-based, and semi-structured.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
